key_risks.title: NVIDIA's long-term financial structure includes $8,463 million in net long-term debt as of January 26, 2025, with maturi
key_risks.claim: NVIDIA's long-term financial structure includes $8,463 million in net long-term debt as of January 26, 2025, with maturities extending beyond ten years, and a $575 million commercial paper program with no outstanding balance, while key risks involve meeting evolving industry needs, intense competition, and managing supply-demand mismatches due to long manufacturing lead times. Recently, the company significantly increased capital expenditures to $3.4 billion in fiscal year 2025 from $1.1 billion in fiscal year 2024, with further increases expected, and noted that a 1% excise tax on share repurchases was not material for fiscal years 2025 and 2024.
key_risks.summary: NVIDIA's long-term financial structure includes $8,463 million in net long-term debt as of January 26, 2025, with maturities extending beyond ten years, and a $575 million commercial paper program with no outstanding balance, while key risks involve meeting evolving industry needs, intense competition, and managing supply-demand mismatches due to long manufacturing lead times. Recently, the company significantly increased capital expenditures to $3.4 billion in fiscal year 2025 from $1.1 billion in fiscal year 2024, with further increases expected, and noted that a 1% excise tax on share repurchases was not material for fiscal years 2025 and 2024.

key_risks.title: NVIDIA's long-term financial structure as of October 26, 2025, includes $8,467 million in net debt, with $7,468 million
key_risks.claim: NVIDIA's long-term financial structure as of October 26, 2025, includes $8,467 million in net debt, with $7,468 million classified as long-term, and $3.2 billion in unrecognized tax benefits. The company also maintains a $575 million commercial paper program, currently with no outstanding balance, and holds various marketable securities. In the recent quarter, there were no material changes to contractual obligations or foreign exchange rate risks, and no material impact from climate-related business trends or sustainability regulations.

final_investment_thesis.thesis: Offsetting disclosures indicate nvidia's long-term financial structure includes $8,463 million in net long-term debt as of january 26, 2025, with maturities extending beyond ten years, and a $575 million commercial paper program with no outstanding balance, while key risks involve meeting evolving industry needs, intense competition, and managing supply-demand mismatches due to long manufacturing lead times. recently, the company significantly increased capital expenditures to $3.4 billion in fiscal year 2025 from $1.1 billion in fiscal year 2024, with further increases expected, and noted that a 1% excise tax on share repurchases was not material for fiscal years 2025 and 2024..
final_investment_thesis.uncertainty_statement: The conclusion remains uncertainty-aware because some signals are only partially supported or coverage is incomplete.

In the recent quarter, there were no material changes to contractual obligations or foreign exchange rate risks, and no material impact from climate-related business trends or sustainability regulations.

excluded_claims.title: NVIDIA Corporation, incorporated in Delaware with its common stock traded on The Nasdaq Global Select Market, designates
excluded_claims.claim: NVIDIA Corporation, incorporated in Delaware with its common stock traded on The Nasdaq Global Select Market, designates its fiscal year by the year in which it ends. A recent material event on March 2, 2026, involved the Compensation Committee establishing the Variable Compensation Plan for Fiscal Year 2027, setting performance goals based on fiscal year 2027 revenue and defining target award opportunities for named executive officers.
